---
title: "City2Graph"
---

::: {.lead}
The City2Graph tab builds *heterogeneous urban graphs* from real-world data:
buildings, streets, amenities, and transit, connected by morphological,
proximity, and metapath relationships. It targets urban analytics and GeoAI
workflows.
:::

The root **City2Graph** panel is a status header. It reports the active object's
type (OSMnx network, City2Graph features, tessellation, morphological graph, or
travel graph) and its feature/node/edge counts. OSMnx street networks can be
used directly by every City2Graph operation, so the two tabs are designed to be
used together.

## Panels in this tab

Each panel of the addon has its own reference page:

| Panel | What it does |
| --- | --- |
| [Data Import](data.qmd) | Acquire the raw layers: Overture/OSMnx features, local files, GTFS transit. |
| [Urban Morphology](morphology.qmd) | Build tessellations and morphological building/street graphs. |
| [Transportation](transportation.qmd) | Turn a loaded GTFS feed into travel-summary and OD graphs. |
| [Mobility (OD Matrix)](mobility.qmd) | Build graphs from an external origin–destination matrix. |
| [Graph Tools](graph-tools.qmd) | Clip, filter, isochrone, and largest-component utilities. |
| [Proximity Graphs](proximity.qmd) | Spatial networks (KNN, radius, Delaunay…) over feature objects. |
| [Metapath Analysis](metapaths.qmd) | Connect amenities through the street network. |
| [GeoAI Export](export.qmd) | Export graphs for GNN training (JSON/GraphML, PyG loader). |

## Header status fields

When a compatible object is active, the header reports:

| Field | Meaning |
| --- | --- |
| **Object** | Name of the active object. |
| **Features** | Feature count for City2Graph feature objects. |
| **Nodes / Edges** | Node and edge counts for graph objects. |
| **Type** | Detected type: OSMnx Street Network, Urban Tessellation, Morphological Graph, or Travel Graph. |

If nothing compatible is selected, the header invites you to import data with the
panels below.

## Typical workflow

1. In **[Data Import](data.qmd)**, define an area and download buildings,
   segments, and places; load a GTFS feed if you need transit.
2. Build a **[Morphological Graph](morphology.qmd)**, generate
   **[Proximity Graphs](proximity.qmd)** over amenities, or run
   **[Metapath Analysis](metapaths.qmd)** to connect amenities along the street
   network. For transit, use **[Transportation](transportation.qmd)** or
   **[Mobility](mobility.qmd)**.
3. Refine the result with **[Graph Tools](graph-tools.qmd)** (keep the largest
   component, clip, isochrone).
4. Visualize in Blender (city2graph layers carry per-type attributes) and/or
   **[export](export.qmd)** for GNN training.

::: {.callout-tip}
Most operations align their output with the **active OSMnx graph** when one is
present, so downloaded features, dual graphs, bridges, and metapaths all share
the same coordinate space as the street network.
:::
